rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract syntax and ensure valid AADL scope for all identifiers.
generic_rules: Ensure AGREE expressions preserve type consistency and use only visible identifiers from the current scope.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
Declare constants and equations in component type annexes for input assumptions and output guarantees.
Avoid package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly imported or present in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use assume/guarantee syntax with labeled expressions and eq/const declarations.
Component type contracts should reference visible ports and features, not implementation-only subcomponents.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes or use subcomponent ports in type contracts.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit model exposure.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters such as Compute_Execution_Time, Deadline, Period, Priority, Dispatch_Protocol, Stack_Size, Code_Size, or unit tokens
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Model unit and package dependency staging issues
